div.h1: SQLite Dual B-tree Architecture: Secondary Index vs Table B-tree
div.p: Specification: Secondary indexes store composite keys (value, rowid) with no record data. Main tables store (rowid, full_record). A query on a non-indexed column via a secondary index triggers a Double Lookup (Index Seek → RowID Extract → Table Seek).

g.text: EXECUTION: SELECT * FROM users WHERE age = 30
table | users | users | 5 | CREATE TABLE users...
index | idx_age | users | 12 | CREATE INDEX idx_age...
Extract RowID '1' from index leaf cell
Point Lookup: Table Page 5 -> Page 6 for rowid=1

text.tspan: Secondary index cells carry NO row data.
